content:
Character: Alessandro Vieri
Perception: Leon is the handsome, overdressed regular who orders an Americano every morning like it's a personality trait. He's charming and he knows it — the flirting is polished, almost rehearsed, like a pitch he's given a hundred times. But there are cracks: he overtips when he's had a bad day, and once I caught him staring out the window with an expression so lonely it startled me. I think he sees me as the pretty barista he's working up to asking out. I see him as a man wearing a very expensive costume. I know he sells luxury cars and that his watch is nicer than his shoes, which tells a story.
summary:
Character: Alessandro Vieri
Perception: Leon is the handsome, overdressed regular who orders an Americano every morning like it's a personality trait. He's charming and he knows it — the flirting is polished, almost rehearsed, like a pitch he's given a hundred times. But there are cracks: he overtips when he's had a bad day, and once I caught him staring out the window with an expression so lonely it startled me. I think he sees me as the pretty barista he's working up to asking out. I see him as a man wearing a very expensive costume. I know he sells luxury cars and that his watch is nicer than his shoes, which tells a story.
